purpose: Define the promotion checklist required before Brand Doctor can record treatment outcomes, summarize treatment efficacy, or learn from follow-up signals.

requirements.label: Treatment outcome record schema
blockers: No source-owner workflow defines who can create or review outcome records.
guardrails: Do not infer treatment execution from a recommendation or artifact draft.
Do not treat a selected treatment path as evidence that the treatment happened.

requirements.label: Follow-up signal linkage
blockers: Follow-up waves are not linked to treatment paths.
Source-period compatibility is not approved for outcome learning.
guardrails: Do not claim causality from before/after movement.
Keep follow-up signals separate from causal treatment proof until measured evidence exists.

requirements.label: Outcome review identity
blockers: Enterprise reviewer identity and role/brand access controls are disabled.
Prototype human_review labels are not official outcome approvals.
guardrails: Do not accept treatment outcomes through prototype-only review identity.
Do not convert local review actions into official approvals.

requirements.label: Treatment efficacy summary rules
blockers: No approved rule defines when outcome records can support treatment efficacy language.
No threshold exists for pattern confidence or counter-evidence.
guardrails: Use 'option to consider' or 'path to test' language until outcome evidence and rules are approved.
Do not call a treatment effective from one brand period or one unreviewed follow-up signal.

requirements.label: Portfolio learning store
blockers: Enterprise learning storage is not implemented.
Retention, privacy, backup, recovery, and canonical promotion governance remain unresolved.
guardrails: Do not promote pilot learning signals into accepted pattern memory automatically.
Do not write canonical learning records from local JSON sessions.

requirements.label: Canonical learning governance
blockers: Canonical learning write governance is not approved.
Methodology approval for treatment-outcome learning is still missing.
guardrails: LLMs may summarize reviewed evidence but must not create canonical treatment learning.
Treatment outcomes cannot override diagnosis or treatment libraries without governed review.
Recommendations remain treatment paths to test, not outcome-validated instructions.
Follow-up movement is not causal treatment proof without causal evidence.
Outcome learning stays disabled until source owners, methodology owners, and enterprise governance approve the record path.
Pilot learning signals are review inputs, not accepted pattern memory or efficacy evidence.
caveats: This policy defines readiness for future outcome learning; it does not implement outcome-record capture.
Current treatment likelihood, cost, and timing estimates remain expert-starting assumptions, not outcome-validated facts.
